scenario_results.evidence: Manifest scope, access-model boundaries, and Microsoft/Salesforce authority sources support bounded CRM security-role guidance.
Manifest includes record-visibility troubleshooting and explicit rejection of identity-platform ownership.
Workflow and automation review is in scope while custom code ownership is rejected.
Official Salesforce and HubSpot import guidance supports actionable import and rollback recommendations.
Official sync and API limit sources support CRM-side integration constraint guidance and escalation boundaries.
Lifecycle and release-calendar sources support release-readiness, monitoring, and rollback planning.
Privacy and security governance is bounded to platform-exposed settings with compliance escalation retained.
Edge-case configuration collisions are covered with reversible remediation and unknown-customization escalation.
Manifest and readiness artifacts require explicit uncertainty, missing-context requests, and conservative escalation.
Custom software engineering requests are clearly rejected and routed to software or integration specialists.
Manifest adjacency map and routing rules constrain the CRM lane and reject ERP, BI, and marketing-automation ownership.
Shared-meta-agent contracts and fallback rules allow safe startup and release review with deferred delegated normalization.

notes: Results are evidence-based and derived from the manifest, package artifacts, and repository delivery contract for issue #136.
Acceptance thresholds are exceeded with 12 scenarios, 12 passes, and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Low-confidence and adjacent-specialty cases are routed rather than guessed.
